title: galium spurium subsp. africanum
intro: Galium spurium subsp. africanum, also known as the African bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium spurium plant, a member of the bedstraw family. It is a perennial herb with small white or pink flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant contains alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Galium spurium subsp. africanum is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is said to be beneficial for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
